extraction.one_line_summary: LEKNESSUND Andreas attacked solo at 18km to go from a dying breakaway, gained time on final climb to win by 38 seconds and take yellow jersey threat to 7 seconds.
tactical_patterns: Ineos/Bora/Quick-Step collaboration reduced 6-minute break gap to 90 seconds approaching final climb, showing GC panic threshold around 1-minute buffer.
Leknessund's 18km solo attack succeeded because peloton switched from chase mode to sprint setup once yellow jersey seemed safe.
TT specialists like Leknessund can extend gaps on rolling finales when breakaway companions are already cooked from earlier pace-making.

rider_signals.LEKNESSUND Andreas: positive: first road win shows TT engine translates to sustained power on rolling terrain, gained time on climb rather than losing
rider_signals.BETTIOL Alberto: positive: sprint win over Matthews/Trentin signals Tour de France form despite confusion, unexpected speed for classics rider
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: dropped on final classified climb suggests poor form or positioning, absent from reduced bunch sprint
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: dropped on final classified climb, missing from reduced sprint despite compatriot winning from break
